prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
(register_integration_entity source_endpoint_1)
(bind_credential_to_entity source_endpoint_1 credential_a)
(bind_file_descriptor_to_entity source_endpoint_1 file_descriptor_1)
(confirm_file_descriptor_binding source_endpoint_1 file_descriptor_1)
(attach_transient_artifact_to_source_channel source_endpoint_1 source_channel_1 transient_artifact_1)
(commit_transient_to_source_channel source_endpoint_1 source_channel_1 file_descriptor_1 transient_artifact_1)
(release_file_descriptor source_endpoint_1 file_descriptor_1)
(register_integration_entity destination_endpoint_1)
(bind_credential_to_entity destination_endpoint_1 credential_b)
(bind_file_descriptor_to_entity destination_endpoint_1 file_descriptor_1)
(confirm_file_descriptor_binding destination_endpoint_1 file_descriptor_1)
(attach_transient_artifact_to_destination_channel destination_endpoint_1 destination_channel_1 transient_artifact_1)
(commit_transient_to_destination_channel destination_endpoint_1 destination_channel_1 file_descriptor_1 transient_artifact_1)
(release_file_descriptor destination_endpoint_1 file_descriptor_1)
(assemble_storage_resource source_endpoint_1 destination_endpoint_1 source_channel_1 destination_channel_1 external_storage_resource_1)
(bind_file_descriptor_to_entity source_endpoint_1 file_descriptor_1)
(attach_payload_to_storage_resource external_storage_resource_1 source_endpoint_1 file_descriptor_1)
(release_file_descriptor source_endpoint_1 file_descriptor_1)
(register_integration_entity orchestrator_service_1)
(bind_credential_to_entity orchestrator_service_1 credential_c)
(bind_file_descriptor_to_entity orchestrator_service_1 file_descriptor_1)
(confirm_file_descriptor_binding orchestrator_service_1 file_descriptor_1)
(register_payload_with_orchestrator_and_bind_resource orchestrator_service_1 file_payload_a external_storage_resource_1)
(claim_payload_for_processing orchestrator_service_1 file_payload_a external_storage_resource_1 file_descriptor_1)
(attach_processor_to_orchestrator orchestrator_service_1 processor_1)
(assign_connection_slot orchestrator_service_1 connection_slot_1)
(start_payload_processing_stage1 orchestrator_service_1 processor_1 connection_slot_1 file_payload_a external_storage_resource_1)
(attach_validator_to_orchestrator orchestrator_service_1 validator_1)
(run_validation orchestrator_service_1 validator_1 file_payload_a external_storage_resource_1)
(finalize_orchestrator_activation orchestrator_service_1)
(consume_operation_ticket_for_entity orchestrator_service_1 operation_ticket_1 file_descriptor_1)
(finalize_orchestrator_activation_with_ticket orchestrator_service_1 credential_c operation_ticket_1)
(release_file_descriptor orchestrator_service_1 file_descriptor_1)
(release_connection_slot orchestrator_service_1 connection_slot_1)
(detach_processor_from_orchestrator orchestrator_service_1 processor_1)
(detach_validator_from_orchestrator orchestrator_service_1 validator_1)
(register_integration_entity orchestrator_service_2)
(bind_credential_to_entity orchestrator_service_2 credential_c)
(bind_file_descriptor_to_entity orchestrator_service_2 file_descriptor_1)
(confirm_file_descriptor_binding orchestrator_service_2 file_descriptor_1)
(register_payload_with_orchestrator_and_bind_resource orchestrator_service_2 file_payload_b external_storage_resource_1)
(claim_payload_for_processing orchestrator_service_2 file_payload_b external_storage_resource_1 file_descriptor_1)
(attach_processor_to_orchestrator orchestrator_service_2 processor_1)
(assign_connection_slot orchestrator_service_2 connection_slot_1)
(start_payload_processing_stage1 orchestrator_service_2 processor_1 connection_slot_1 file_payload_b external_storage_resource_1)
(attach_validator_to_orchestrator orchestrator_service_2 validator_1)
(run_validation orchestrator_service_2 validator_1 file_payload_b external_storage_resource_1)
(finalize_orchestrator_activation orchestrator_service_2)
(consume_operation_ticket_for_entity orchestrator_service_2 operation_ticket_1 file_descriptor_1)
(finalize_orchestrator_activation_with_ticket orchestrator_service_2 credential_c operation_ticket_1)
(release_file_descriptor orchestrator_service_2 file_descriptor_1)
(release_connection_slot orchestrator_service_2 connection_slot_1)
(detach_processor_from_orchestrator orchestrator_service_2 processor_1)
(detach_validator_from_orchestrator orchestrator_service_2 validator_1)
(activate_source_endpoint source_endpoint_1 external_storage_resource_1)
(activate_destination_endpoint destination_endpoint_1 external_storage_resource_1)
(bind_file_descriptor_to_entity source_endpoint_1 file_descriptor_1)
(consume_operation_ticket_for_entity source_endpoint_1 operation_ticket_1 file_descriptor_1)
(finalize_source_activation source_endpoint_1 credential_a operation_ticket_1)
(release_file_descriptor source_endpoint_1 file_descriptor_1)
(bind_file_descriptor_to_entity destination_endpoint_1 file_descriptor_1)
(consume_operation_ticket_for_entity destination_endpoint_1 operation_ticket_1 file_descriptor_1)
(finalize_destination_activation destination_endpoint_1 credential_b operation_ticket_1)
message.reasoning:
The problem involves integrating source_endpoint_1, destination_endpoint_1, orchestrator_service_1, and orchestrator_service_2. The goal is to have all four entity_integrated.


## Understanding Integration  
- The task requires integrating four entities: source_endpoint_1, destination_endpoint_1, orchestrator_service_1, and orchestrator_service_2.  
- The goal is to ensure all four components work together seamlessly as a unified system.

## Assessing Initial Resources  
- The system starts with credentials a, b, c; file_descriptor_1; connection_slot_1; and transient_artifact_1 available.  
- Source_endpoint_1 uses source_channel_1, and destination_endpoint_1 uses destination_channel_1, with external_storage_resource_1 accessible.  

## Examining Entity Details  
- Orchestrator_service_1 manages file_payload_a, while orchestrator_service_2 manages file_payload_b, both sharing external_storage_resource_1.  
- Verifier_a is available for orchestrator_service_1, but verifier_b's availability for orchestrator_service_2 is unclear, with validator_1 and processor_1 also present.  

## Outlining Integration Steps  
- Integration requires registering all entities—source_endpoint_1, destination_endpoint_1, orchestrator_service_1, and orchestrator_service_2—before binding resources.  
- Binding credentials and file_descriptor_1 (only one available) suggests a need for sequential binding, use, and release due to limited shared resources.  

## Analyzing Resource Constraints  
- With only three credentials for four entities and one file_descriptor_1, resource sharing is necessary, potentially complicating the integration.  
- Operation_ticket_1 and limited connection_slot_1 indicate sequential processing is likely required for activation and finalization.

## Analyzing Resource Usage  
- Limited resources like file_descriptor_1 and connection_slot_1 require careful sequencing, with actions like bind and release to manage availability.  
- External_storage_resource_1 is shared by both orchestrators, potentially needing assembly once for use by multiple entities.  

## Exploring Activation Paths  
- Endpoints require storage reserved and payload attached to reach ready_for_activation, involving steps like assemble_storage_resource.  
- Orchestrators follow similar paths, using consume_operation_ticket_for_entity and finalize_activation, with context verification steps like apply_context_verifier.  

## Planning Integration Sequence  
- Prepare endpoints first by registering, binding credentials, and ensuring storage and payload readiness before activating.  
- Sequence actions to minimize conflicts, such as processing endpoints before orchestrators, given shared resources like processor_1 and validator_1.

## Evaluating Slot Usage  
- Only one connection_slot_1 is available, and assigning it to source_endpoint_1 creates conflicts for preparing destination_endpoint_1, as both need it.  
- An alternative path using attach_transient_artifact_to_source_channel avoids slot requirements, setting channel_staged and endpoint_ready without preparation.  

## Mapping Transient Artifact Path  
- The transient artifact path involves attaching transient_artifact_1 to the source channel, requiring file_descriptor_1, and commits it to prepare the endpoint, releasing transient_artifact_1 temporarily.  
- This path allows sequential processing for source_endpoint_1 and destination_endpoint_1, using transient_artifact_1 and file_descriptor_1 without needing the slot.  

## Addressing File Descriptor Conflicts  
- After committing for source, file_descriptor_1 remains bound, requiring release for destination_endpoint_1, then rebinding later for source actions like attach_payload_to_storage.  
- This sequencing ensures resource availability, with file_descriptor_1 shared sequentially between endpoints, minimizing conflicts.

## Assessing Orchestrator Activation  
- Orchestrator_service_1 requires verified status and no config applied to finalize activation, leading to committed and ready_for_activation states.  
- Consuming operation_ticket_1 for orchestrator_service_1 authorizes it, using file_descriptor_1, resulting in an integrated state with resources still bound.

## Managing Resource Availability  
- Resources like processor_1, validator_1, and connection_slot_1 remain bound to orchestrator_service_1 after integration, requiring detachment for orchestrator_service_2.  
- Releasing file_descriptor_1 from orchestrator_service_1 makes it available for binding to orchestrator_service_2, ensuring sequential resource use.

## Preparing Orchestrator Service 2  
- Registering and binding credential_c to orchestrator_service_2 enables setup, reusing available credentials for efficiency.  
- Binding file_descriptor_1 and registering file_payload_b with external_storage_resource_1 prepares orchestrator_service_2 for further activation steps.

## Sequencing Endpoint Operations  
- The process begins with registering and binding credentials and file descriptors for source_endpoint_1, followed by attaching and committing a transient artifact to the source channel.  
- File descriptor release from source_endpoint_1 enables binding to destination_endpoint_1, ensuring sequential resource availability for destination channel preparation.  

## Detailing Full Resource Flow  
- Destination_endpoint_1 follows a similar setup, releasing the file descriptor after preparation, which is later reused for assembling storage resources without needing it.  
- Source_endpoint_1 rebinds the file descriptor for attaching the payload to storage, maintaining resource continuity.  

## Preparing Orchestrator Services  
- Orchestrator_service_1 registration involves binding credential_c and the file descriptor, previously released from source_endpoint_1, ensuring resource reuse.  
- Registering file_payload_a with orchestrator_service_1 and external_storage_resource_1 finalizes its setup for further processing.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
